data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: The site design considers the time needed to acquire targets on a webpage, with larger and closer buttons for ease of use.
data.technical_spec: Larger clickable areas in key navigation and action sections.

data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: The interface minimizes choice overload by providing intuitive and limited options to encourage decision-making.
data.technical_spec: Dropdown menus and progressive disclosure techniques.

data.title: Gestalt Principles
data.description: Effective use of proximity, similarity, and continuity in arranging elements, fostering a clean, organized visual hierarchy.
data.technical_spec: Consistent spacing and pattern recognition throughout the interface.

data.title: Law of Proximity
data.description: Related items are placed close together to create a clear association between elements.
data.technical_spec: Groupings in user interfaces reflect task-related activities, organizing content logically.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Limiting the user’s cognitive load by grouping content and information to span no more than 7±2 concepts.
data.technical_spec: Clear categorization and summarization of information.
